summary: Jam‑heavy second set with epic Shakedown Street and flawless Terrapin
blurb: The show delivers a jam‑filled second set highlighted by an extended Shakedown Street and a pristine Terrapin Station suite. Jerry’s guitar smokes on Row Jimmy while Brent’s piano adds perfect mellowness throughout. Overall the performance is strong with standout moments in both sets.
review:
December 9, 1979 at St. Louis’ Kiel Auditorium captures the Dead in peak ’79 form – a setlist that swings from tight rock‑fest energy into deep, exploratory jamming. The first set rocks hard with Row Jimmy’s scorching slide work and a tight rhythm section that propels Lazy Lightning into Supplication. The second half is where the night truly shines: an almost 14‑minute Shakedown Street stretches out, flowing seamlessly into a flawless Terrapin Station suite (Terrapin > Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance) and a soaring Space that feeds directly into Black Peter. Brent Mydland’s piano provides a mellow yet powerful backbone throughout, and Phil’s “Phil‑heavy” low end anchors the mix while Bob adds solid lead vocals on several numbers. Jerry’s guitar is often described as “smoking” and “excellent,” though his vocals wobble a bit at times.

For listeners seeking the definitive version of this concert, the Miller soundboard (gd79-12-09.sbd.miller.27751.sbeok.flacf) is the clear choice – it’s praised for crisp, balanced audio that hides the venue’s acoustic quirks and captures every nuance of the jam‑heavy performance. While an audience tape exists, its out‑of‑phase, fast‑playing nature makes it a tough listen compared to the pristine SBD. In short, this show is a must‑listen for fans who love the transitionary period between the late ’70s groove and the emerging polish of the early ’80s.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across multiple recordings: reviewers call the show 'stellar', 'balanced', and highlight an 'epic' Shakedown Street, Row Jimmy, and Terrapin Station suite. Two soundboard analyses are wholly positive, while the lone audience tape is mixed but does not detract from performance praise.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture – crisp, clear mix that balances vocals, Brent’s piano and the jam‑heavy sections while hiding venue acoustic issues.

key_highlights: Epic, jam‑heavy second set anchored by a 14‑minute Shakedown Street and flawless Terrapin Station suite.
Row Jimmy shines early with scorching guitar and tight rhythm section.
Brent Mydland’s piano adds perfect mellowness throughout, especially on the Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance transition.
Recommended Miller SBD is crisp and balanced, delivering a superior listening experience.

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar is described as smoking and excellent, delivering scorching slide work on Row Jimmy; vocals are occasionally shaky but overall solid.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a punchy, low‑end presence that anchors the mix; strong backup vocals especially noted on Shakedown Street.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers solid lead vocals and helps make Shakedown Street a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work receives glowing praise for perfect mellowness and raised vocal/instrumental quality; his piano shines throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums contribute the classic Terrapin/Shakedown‑era thump, offering solid rhythmic drive in the dual‑drum setup.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady, powerful drumming alongside Mickey, reinforcing the groove and supporting the extended jams.
